title: Front-end Architect
tagline: I architect Angular and React frontends for telecom and education clients — micro-frontends, performance budgets, and design systems that scale past a single team.
metaDescription: Annamalai Kathiresan — Senior Front-end Architect. Angular, React, micro-frontends at Tata Communications, Comcast, ETS, and Verizon.

about.prose: I'm a front-end architect with 5 years shipping UI for enterprise clients. Most of my work sits in Angular — module federation, lazy loading, NgRx state — with React and React Native on select projects. I care about bundle size, Core Web Vitals, and making complex enterprise workflows feel effortless for the people using them every day.

items.title: Tata Communications — Micro-frontend Platform
items.context: A licensed Angular product suite where multiple teams ship features into one shell application.
items.built: Module federation architecture with independently deployable remotes, shared design tokens, route-level code splitting, and a lazy-loading strategy tuned per feature module.
items.outcome: Teams deploy their modules independently without shell releases, and initial bundle size dropped enough to hit the product's load-time budget on low-bandwidth enterprise networks.

items.title: Comcast — Bulk Operations UI
items.context: Field operations teams were onboarding customer accounts one at a time through a slow manual flow.
items.built: Bulk Invite and Bulk Modem Restart flows — NgRx-orchestrated wizards with optimistic UI, RxJS-batched requests, and inline per-row error recovery so one failure never blocked a batch.
items.outcome: 75% faster onboarding for bulk operations; the flow became the default path for field teams.

items.title: ETS — TOEFL Learn Mobile App
items.context: TOEFL exam preparation app that had to ship simultaneously on iOS and Android with a small team.
items.built: React Native component library with a single design language, shared navigation and state layers, and platform-specific overrides kept to the edges.
items.outcome: 90% code reuse between iOS and Android builds — one team shipped both stores on the same release cycle.

items.title: Verizon — Operations Dashboard
items.context: Internal ops teams tracking service metrics on dashboards that were slow to first paint.
items.built: Angular + PrimeNG dashboards rebuilt around lazy routes, OnPush change detection, virtualized tables, and an Adobe Analytics event layer for usage insight.
items.outcome: 30% reduction in initial load time and smooth interaction on data-dense views.

highlights: Lead front-end architect on licensed Angular products for Tata Communications
Own the module federation strategy, shared design system, and front-end CI/CD via GitHub Actions
Drive accessibility compliance and code review standards across the UI team
Shipped UI for Verizon, ETS, and Comcast under tight enterprise release cycles
Built Bulk Invite and Bulk Modem Restart — 75% onboarding improvement at Comcast
Made performance profiling and bundle analysis a standing practice on every codebase I owned

contact.message: Open to front-end architecture roles, design system work, or talking through a hard UI performance problem.
